title: Transcriptomic insights into lobular breast cancer biology and patient outcomes: analysis of the MINDACT clinical trial.
abstract: Invasive lobular carcinoma (ILC) is the second most common subtype of breast cancer after invasive breast cancer of no special type (IBC-NST). This retrospective analysis of the MINDACT trial investigated transcriptomic differences between estrogen receptor-positive/HER2-negative (ER+/HER2-) ILC versus ER+/HER2- IBC-NST, classic and non-classic ER+/HER2- ILC, and, recurring and non-recurring ER+/HER2- ILC in patients with a low genomic risk and either a low (cL/gL) or high clinical risk (cH/gL).
